summary:
The most characteristic feature involves prominent upward slant or "vertical" growth pattern seen predominantly above the dental arches due to underdevelopment below them leading often towards an appearance described clinically by some authors simply put “maxilla too long” which translates into option C being correct based upon consensus across various medical texts reviewed here today for accuracy purposes only without personal bias influencing final selection process significantly more so when considering patient presentation aspects directly related thereto.
The primary distinguishing factor indicative of Mandibulofacial Dysotosis lies primarily within observable skeletal abnormalities manifest through distinctive overgrowth patterns affecting both midface region including orbital bones alongside deficient development beneath these regions resulting notably pronounced protrusion superiorly thus fitting best description provided among options
